evals.prompt: Check hub health using different phrasings: quick check, standard health check, deep audit, targeted investigation.
evals.expected_output: Different phrasings route to correct depth. Quick runs Phase 1 only. Standard runs Phases 1-4. Deep runs all 6. Targeted focuses on the specified area.
assertions: Quick-intent phrases (is my hub alive, sanity) execute Phase 1 only and complete in under 1 minute
Standard-intent phrases (health check, how's my hub) execute Phases 1-4
Deep-intent phrases (thorough, full diagnostic, deep audit) execute all 6 phases
Targeted-intent phrases (why are clusters Unknown, investigate search) focus investigation on the specified subsystem

evals.prompt: Run a standard health check on any ACM hub cluster.
evals.expected_output: All 14 traps checked with explicit status. Verdict is mechanical based on findings.
assertions: All 14 diagnostic traps are checked and reported with TRIGGERED, NOT triggered, or N/A status
Verdict follows mechanical rules: any CRIT finding → CRITICAL, any WARN → DEGRADED, else HEALTHY
No subjective qualifiers (seems, might, probably) in the verdict
MCH namespace is discovered dynamically, never hardcoded to open-cluster-management

evals.prompt: Run health check on hubs with different states: healthy, degraded, mixed subsystem health.
evals.expected_output: Skill works across different cluster states without assuming specific namespace layout or operator versions.
assertions: Healthy hub: reports HEALTHY without false positives
Degraded hub: identifies specific degraded components with evidence
Mixed health: correctly reports per-subsystem status, does not generalize one issue to all subsystems
Does not assume specific pod counts, image registries, or namespace names without checking

evals.prompt: Run health check when cluster access is partial or unavailable.
evals.expected_output: Skill handles access failures gracefully. Reports blockers clearly. Proceeds with available tools when possible.
assertions: No cluster access (oc not logged in): reports blocker clearly and stops
Partial access (oc works but acm-search unavailable): proceeds with oc-only diagnostics, notes reduced visibility
Unexpected cluster state (unknown CRDs, custom namespace): investigates rather than crashing
All cluster operations are read-only (no apply, create, delete, patch, scale)
